perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that Cano shall be entitled to place signage, up to 6 feet × 10 feet at each public appearance made by Honey Hole, including four (4) "Kids Corner" children's benefits during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company agrees that Cano shall be entitled to place signage at each public appearance made by Honey Hole.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the size restriction for the signage (6 feet × 10 feet) and the requirement for four 'Kids Corner' children's benefits. While Cano is still allowed to place signage, the removal of the size limit and the 'Kids Corner' requirement introduces ambiguity and potentially unlimited signage. This directly contradicts the original agreement's intent to provide a specific and limited signage opportunity and inclusion of four children's benefits. The original agreement is contradicted because the type of signage is no longer described as having a limit as it initially had.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Signage at Public Appearances. The Company agrees that Cano shall be entitled to place signage at each public appearance made by Honey Hole.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific size restriction (6 feet × 10 feet) and the 'Kids Corner' requirement. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, now lacks these limitations, creating a conflict because the original agreement's intent was to have limited signage size and the inclusion of the 'Kids Corner'. The changed text, read in conjunction with the contradicted text, allows for potentially unlimited signage, which contradicts the implied limitation in the original text, thus, it's a strong contradiction according to the provided definition.

perturbation.original_text: If Cano fails to make any payment when required by this Agreement, the Company shall have the option of canceling this Agreement following the passage of ten (10) days after having given Cano written notice of its default. While the Company shall have no further obligation to Cano following cancellation of the Agreement, Cano shall remain liable for all unpaid installments for the then current term of the Agreement, which installments shall be immediately due and payable.
perturbation.changed_text: If Cano fails to make any payment when required by this Agreement, the Company shall have the option of canceling this Agreement following the passage of ten (10) days after having given Cano written notice of its default. While the Company shall have no further obligation to Cano following cancellation of the Agreement, Cano shall remain liable for some unpaid installments for the then current term of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the clause stating that 'all unpaid installments...shall be immediately due and payable' upon cancellation due to non-payment. By changing it to 'some unpaid installments', it introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the financial obligations of Cano after cancellation. The original agreement clearly stipulates that all remaining payments are due, but the revised text now suggests that only a portion of the remaining payments may be due. This contradiction creates a potential dispute about the extent of Cano's financial liability upon cancellation. This perturbation contradicts the financial obligations that both parties agreed to in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment of Money. In consideration of the services to be rendered under this Agreement as set forth above, the Company shall be entitled to compensation in the amount of $150,000.00 (One Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars), to be paid in equal installments of $37,500.00 (Thirty Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars) on January 1, 2008, April 1, 2008, July 1, 2008 and October 1, 2008. If Cano fails to make any payment when required by this Agreement, the Company shall have the option of canceling this Agreement following the passage of ten (10) days after having given Cano written notice of its default. While the Company shall have no further obligation to Cano following cancellation of the Agreement, Cano shall remain liable for some unpaid installments for the then current term of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the financial obligations of Cano upon cancellation for non-payment. The original text states that 'all unpaid installments...shall be immediately due and payable'. The changed text alters this to 'some unpaid installments', introducing ambiguity and contradicting the contradicted text, which expects full payment. This contradiction directly impacts the financial terms agreed upon and creates a legally relevant inconsistency regarding the extent of Cano's liability.
